auth_gate.title: Unlock Personal Jarvis
auth_gate.subtitle: Enter the Control Key of this Jarvis instance.
auth_gate.invalid: That Control Key was not accepted.
auth_gate.unavailable: Could not reach Jarvis. Try again.
auth_gate.where_title: Where do I find the Control Key?
auth_gate.where_hint: It was never sent to you — it is generated automatically for this Jarvis install. Open Jarvis on the computer it is installed on: under API Keys there is a dedicated key section named after your assistant (e.g. "Nico Key") where you can show it, copy it, or replace it with one of your own.
auth_gate.where_hint_server: On a headless server it is stored in the .control_api_key file inside the Jarvis data folder.

file_toast.drag_hint: Drag me anywhere — to a folder, a website, a chat

jarvis_dock.aria: {name} — drop a mission here to talk about it
jarvis_dock.hint: Drag a {name}-Agent output here to pull it into the conversation

topbar.restart_missions_running: mission(s) still running — click again to restart anyway
topbar.update_hint: Download the new version and restart {name} automatically to install it
topbar.update_deps_warning: Updated — some components may need a manual reinstall
topbar.update_desktop_warning: Updated — the operating system could not fully repair the app launcher
topbar.update_staged_restart_failed: Update downloaded — restart {name} to finish installing it
topbar.update_rolled_back: The last update could not be installed — {name} was rolled back to the previous version

agent_instructions.subtitle: Your personal preferences for how the assistant works with you — tone, language, defaults, standing facts. Separate from the System Prompt, and it never overrides safety or confirmations.
agent_instructions.empty_hint: Empty - save to clear the file, or start from the template.
agent_instructions.applies_next_turn: Applies on your assistant's next message — no restart needed.

taskbar_view.subtitle: On-screen overlay appearance and dictation behaviour.

bar_persistent.title: Show bar at all times
bar_persistent.description: Keep the bar on screen even when idle. Off: no standby — it only appears when you say your wake word.

follow_cursor.title: Follow the mouse to the active monitor
follow_cursor.description: The bar hops to whichever monitor your mouse is on, keeping the same spot. Off: it stays on one monitor.

mute_music.title: Mute music while dictating
mute_music.description: Mute other apps (Spotify, browser…) while you talk to your assistant, then restore them after. The assistant's own voice is never muted.
mute_music.enabled_toast: Music will mute while you talk to your assistant.

sound_effects.title: Sound effects
sound_effects.description: Play Jarvis's short tones — the chime when you say the wake word, the hang-up tone, and the boot-ready cue. Off: silent; the spoken voice is never affected.

socials.subtitle: Links to our community and profiles.
socials.label: Label

contacts.subtitle: Your personal address book — names, contact details, and a short note per person.
contacts.empty: No contacts yet — add your first one.
contacts.selectHint: Select a contact to see details.
contacts.name: Name
contacts.readmePlaceholder: A short note about this person…

dictionary.title: Dictionary
dictionary.description: Teach speech recognition your words — names, brands, technical terms. Add a word, or fix something it keeps hearing wrong.
dictionary.empty: No words yet — add names or terms the voice recognition should know.
dictionary.applies_note: Changes apply to your next Pipeline transcription — no restart needed. Realtime mode does not use Dictionary corrections.
dictionary.correction_hint_on: Whenever the left side is heard, it is replaced by the right side.
dictionary.correction_hint_off: Add a word so the recognition knows it and spells it exactly like this.
dictionary.misheard_comma_hint: Separate several misheard variants with commas.

voice_state.warming_hint: Getting ready to listen – one moment, you'll be able to speak shortly.

sidebar.apikeys_alert: A provider is set up but not working — open API Keys to fix it
sidebar.plugins_reconnect_alert: A plugin connection expired — open Skills & Tools to reconnect it
sidebar.realtime_browser_hint: Your browser owns the microphone on this device.
sidebar.realtime_browser_unavailable: Realtime Voice is unavailable in this browser
sidebar.realtime_https_required: Browser voice requires HTTPS (or localhost). Open this server through an HTTPS reverse proxy.
sidebar.realtime_microphone_unavailable: This browser cannot access a microphone. Check browser support and device settings.
sidebar.realtime_audio_worklet_unavailable: This browser does not support the required audio processor. Update the browser.
sidebar.realtime_microphone_denied: Microphone access was denied. Allow it in the browser's site settings, then retry.
sidebar.realtime_browser_tts_unavailable: This browser has no usable system voice. The answer is still available as text; connect a TTS provider for audio.
sidebar.realtime_error: Couldn’t start voice. Test the active Realtime provider under API Keys, then retry.

chats_view.title: Chat
chats_view.subtitle: Talk to your assistant or type — both go through the same event bus.
chats_view.empty_subtitle: Say your wake word or type your message below. Your assistant dispatches {name}-Agents, calls tools, and gives you a voice back.
chats_view.warming_subtitle: Your assistant is starting up. You'll be able to speak in a moment, and you can already type below.
chats_view.speak_started: Listening — continue this conversation by voice.
chats_view.speak_unavailable: Voice isn't available in this mode.
chats_view.select_or_new: Pick a conversation on the left, or start a new one.
chats_view.resize_hint: Drag to resize · double-click to reset

subagents_view.title: {name}-Agents
subagents_view.explicit_spawn_hint: {name}-Agents start only when you explicitly ask for one — say e.g. “Spawn an agent for this” or “Do a deep dive with an agent”.

error_class.provider_auth: Provider sign-in invalid or expired — reconnect it in the API-Keys view.
error_class.provider_quota: Provider quota exhausted — missions retry on another provider.
error_class.worker_timeout: The worker hit its time limit.

tasks_view.title: Tasks
tasks_view.subtitle: Persistent task queue — reminders, harness dispatches, tool calls.
tasks_view.empty_hint: Create a scheduled task with the New button — give it a prompt, choose when it runs (once or recurring), and toggle the plugins it may use.

create.title: New scheduled task
create.subtitle: Runs your prompt on a schedule, with the plugins you allow.
create.prompt_placeholder: e.g. Summarize today's calendar and unread mail into a short briefing.
create.plugins_empty: No connected plugins. Connect some under Skills, Plugins & MCPs first.
create.unattended_hint: Write/Full plugins run unattended — this task may send or post without asking you first.
create.announce_placeholder: e.g. Your mission is ready — I opened it in the browser.
create.when_then_hint: Fires every time this happens. You can use {result_uri}, {summary_en} or {mission_id} as placeholders in the text above.

skills_view.title: Skills
skills_view.subtitle: Built-in and user skills with voice and event triggers.
skills_view.delete_body: This skill will be permanently removed:
skills_view.bulk_delete_body: These skills will be permanently removed:
skills_view.select_from_list: Select a skill from the list.
skills_view.builtin_admin_needed: Built-in — admin password required to save
skills_view.admin_password_hint_a: Built-in skills are protected. Enter the admin password set in
skills_view.empty_list_body_c: If that doesn't happen, check the backend logs.
skills_view.search_no_hits: No matches. Try a different term or remove a filter.

run_inspector.title: Run Inspector
run_inspector.subtitle: Per-run forensics: full transcript, timeline, latency, decisions, tools, errors.

sessions_view.title: Transcription
sessions_view.subtitle: Recent voice sessions, turn by turn.
sessions_view.empty_subtitle: After a voice conversation with your assistant, the transcripts appear here.

docs_view.title: Docs
docs_view.subtitle: Project documentation, architecture decisions, and bug log.

mcps_view.title: MCPs
mcps_view.subtitle: Model Context Protocol servers — external tools {name} can call.
mcps_view.intro: {name} manages MCP servers automatically. Activate one with the switch — the connection is checked first. For manual edits open mcp.json.
mcps_view.empty_description: MCP servers are configured exclusively via mcp.json (Claude Desktop format). Edit it directly, or import the full Claude Desktop config.
mcps_view.empty_tip: Tip: Tell {name} later "install MCP X" — it will add it to mcp.json itself.
mcps_view.config_format_hint: Claude Desktop format. Per server: command, args, optional env and enabled. Env values with $-prefix are resolved from the Credential Manager (e.g. "$GMAIL_TOKEN").

board_view.title: Board
board_view.subtitle: Everything you and {name} have done together — fully local.

share.title: Share your stats
share.subtitle: A snapshot of your {name} activity.
share.status_copied: Image copied — paste it (Ctrl/Cmd+V) into your post.
share.status_copy_unsupported: Copy isn't supported here — saved the PNG instead.
share.status_composer: Image copied — paste it into the X composer.
share.status_error: Couldn't generate the image. Try again.
share.status_blocked: Couldn't open X — the image is on your clipboard, open x.com and paste it.

board_view.activity_subtitle: How many words you and {name} exchanged each day.
board_view.activity_heatmap_hint: Each square is a day — the brighter, the busier.
board_view.chart_empty: Not enough data yet to chart.
board_view.categories_subtitle: Every tool {name} ran for you, grouped by purpose.
board_view.categories_empty: No tool usage yet — it fills in as {name} works.
board_view.activity_description: Last 365 days. Voice, chat, tools, tasks and app starts. Each cell = one day.
board_view.ai_profile_description: What {name} has learned about you. Updated evolutionarily on Sundays.
board_view.ai_profile_first_bio_hint: First short bio appears after the first active day, deeper observations on Sundays.
board_view.feedback_noted: Noted. Will affect the next bio.
board_view.tools_chart_empty: No tool usage in this window
board_view.backend_local_only: Federation is disabled (local-only mode). Enable in jarvis.toml: [board.federation] enabled = true.
board_view.no_records_yet: No records yet — they appear automatically once data exists.

profile_view.title: Profile
profile_view.subtitle: Your USER.md, people you know, and pending memory reviews.
profile_view.no_name_yet: {name} doesn't know your name yet. Tell him "My name is …".
profile_view.reviews_disabled_body: This session's fact curator writes straight to the wiki vault and doesn't use a review queue. Candidates show up here once a provider with the classic memory integration is running.
profile_view.hero_tagline: What {name} has learned about you — gathered from conversation, kept in one book.
profile_view.no_user_hint: {name} doesn't know your name yet. Just tell him what to call you — the curator picks up the rest from your conversations over time.
profile_view.section_knowledge_sub: Learned from your conversations, grouped the way {name} thinks about you.
profile_view.hero_sub: Everything here was learned in passing, from real conversations — never asked twice, never guessed.

questions.name: What name shall I write on the first page?
questions.preferred_address: How would you like to be addressed?
questions.primary_language: Which language shall we keep between us?
questions.languages: Which other languages do you live in?
questions.timezone: Which clock do your days run on?
questions.devices: Which devices should I keep in mind?
questions.directness: How blunt may I be with you?
questions.formality: Formal or familiar — how do we talk?
questions.verbosity: Short answers or the full picture?
questions.humor_types: What kind of humor lands with you?
questions.emoji_ok: Do emojis belong in our chats?
questions.focus_mode: How do you guard your focus?
questions.planning_horizon: Do you plan in days, weeks or years?
questions.top_values: What do you refuse to compromise on?
questions.motivations: What gets you out of bed?
questions.feedback_pref: How should I deliver hard truths?

profile_view.people_empty_body: When you tell {name} about family, friends or colleagues, the curator automatically creates a file under data/workspace/people/.
profile_view.person_detail_hint: Pick a person on the left to see details.
profile_view.person_file_hint: Observations and full notes live in people/{0}.md.
profile_view.reviews_empty_body: Every extracted fact was high-confidence and applied directly. When the curator is unsure, candidates land here.
profile_view.raw_empty: (File empty — the curator hasn't written anything yet)
profile_view.raw_editing_hint: Editing the source file directly. The YAML frontmatter between the --- drives the clusters above.
profile_view.raw_frontmatter_warning: Saved — but the YAML frontmatter couldn't be parsed. The clusters stay empty until you fix it.
profile_view.avatar_hint: Click the frame to upload a picture of yourself.

communication.label: Communication
communication.description: How {name} talks to you: directness, humor, formality.

relationship.label: Relationship
relationship.description: How {name} gives feedback, how the dynamic runs.

apikeys_view.title: API Keys & Providers
apikeys_view.subtitle: Connect providers and choose what powers each part of Jarvis.
apikeys_view.shared_key_covered: Already covered by your shared key from this provider family — a dedicated key is optional.
apikeys_view.shared_delete_prefix: This key is also used by:
apikeys_view.shared_delete_suffix: Deleting it disables those providers too.
apikeys_view.jarvis_key_desc: The one key that protects this install — it unlocks {name} in the browser and authenticates local agents.
apikeys_view.voice_engine_desc: How Jarvis listens and talks — the classic pipeline, or a single realtime model.
apikeys_view.mode_needs_key: Realtime needs an API key — add one below to switch.
apikeys_view.mode_desc_pipeline: Three steps: speech-to-text, the Brain thinks, text-to-speech.
apikeys_view.mode_desc_realtime: One model listens and answers in the same live audio stream.
apikeys_view.mode_keys_hint: You only need API keys for the mode you use: Realtime runs on a single realtime-capable key, Pipeline uses its own speech and Brain keys.
apikeys_view.mode_realtime_preview: Research preview — not finished yet. Many tools and features don't work in realtime mode.
apikeys_view.runtime_switching: Restarting the current voice session with the selected engine…
apikeys_view.runtime_idle: No voice session is active. This selection applies to the next one.
apikeys_view.runtime_fallback_pipeline: Current session fell back to Pipeline; Realtime is not serving this call.
apikeys_view.cat_brain_desc: The model that writes your chat and voice answers. The separate Tool Model handles actions and Computer Use.
apikeys_view.cat_stt_desc: How Jarvis turns your speech into text.
apikeys_view.cat_realtime_desc: Full-duplex speech-to-speech through any configured Realtime provider, on desktop or in the browser.
apikeys_view.cat_computer_use_desc: The Tool Model runs your actions: it routes spoken commands, plans Computer-Use on your screen, starts background agents, and drives app controls — independent of your chat Brain.
apikeys_view.cat_subagents_desc: Choose which provider runs heavy background tasks.
apikeys_view.advanced_desc: Optional extras: the team key proxy, telephony, and the knowledge Wiki provider.
apikeys_view.no_providers_in_tier: No providers available in this category.
apikeys_view.model_picker_activate_hint: Activate this provider to choose its voice or model.
apikeys_view.needs_codex: First connect Codex or save API key, then activate
apikeys_view.needs_login: First sign in with Google, then activate
apikeys_view.needs_key: First save API key, then activate
apikeys_view.trademark_notice: Provider and service names and logos are trademarks of their respective owners, shown only to identify the integrations you can connect. No affiliation or endorsement is implied.
apikeys_view.guide_realtime_body: OpenAI: GPT Realtime (the provider default) sounds the most natural; GPT Realtime Mini is the budget pick. Google: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is the current Live flagship. "Provider default" always resolves to the recommended model, so you can simply leave it.
apikeys_view.guide_computer_use_title: Which provider should power the Tool Model?
apikeys_view.guide_computer_use_body: The Tool Model handles every action Jarvis takes for you — including Computer-Use, which reads screenshots of your screen, so it needs a model that understands images; the picker hides text-only models. If unsure, keep your main Brain provider and its default model.
apikeys_view.reco_realtime_why: Gemini Live is fine too, either works.
apikeys_view.reco_subagents_why: flat rate for heavy background work.
apikeys_view.mode_status_unknown: Engine status has not loaded yet - the switch unlocks as soon as the backend answers. If this persists, restart the app.

apikeys_test.integration_ok_note: The provider was reached — only the key/account/model is the blocker.
apikeys_test.integration_bad_note: The provider could not be reached.

apikeys_model.heading: Model
apikeys_model.search_placeholder: Search models or type a custom id…
apikeys_model.source_static_note: Live catalog unavailable — showing the maintained default selection.
apikeys_model.filter_hint_free: Zero-cost models — no API credit needed.
apikeys_model.filter_hint_frontier: When price doesn't matter — the most capable models.
apikeys_model.filter_hint_value: The best performance for the price.

apikeys_cu_model.heading: Tool Model
apikeys_cu_model.cleared: Tool Model reset to your main model
apikeys_cu_model.vision_note: Only models that can see images are listed - Computer-Use reads the screen from screenshots, so a text-only model cannot run it.

permissions.title: macOS permissions
permissions.description: Grant only the system access needed for voice, Computer-Use, global shortcuts, and window control. Jarvis never edits the macOS privacy database.
permissions.load_failed: Could not read the current permission status.
permissions.not_required: No extra desktop privacy permissions are required on this operating system.
permissions.identity_warning: Launch Personal Jarvis from its app bundle before granting access. macOS attaches permissions to the exact app identity.
permissions.restart_required: Restart Personal Jarvis before using the newly granted access.
permissions.restart_failed: Personal Jarvis could not restart. Quit and reopen it manually.

microphone.title: Microphone
microphone.description: Required for wake-word and local voice input.

screen_recording.title: Screen Recording
screen_recording.description: Required for Computer-Use to see every connected display.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: Required for reliable clicks, typing, focus, and window control.

event_posting.title: Input control
event_posting.description: Required for Computer-Use to send mouse and keyboard events.

credential_store.title: Keychain (API keys)
credential_store.description: Stores your API keys encrypted in the macOS Keychain instead of a plain file — that is the Keychain prompt you see when the app starts. Your keys stay on your Mac and are never sent anywhere.

banner.title: Action needed: macOS permissions are missing
banner.hint: Use the buttons to allow access — macOS opens the right System Settings page for each permission. {name} re-checks automatically.

permissions.restart_deferred: Newly granted access turns on with the automatic restart at the end of setup - nothing to do now.

silence_window.title: Thinking pause
silence_window.description: How long {name} waits in silence before it sends what you said. Raise it to give yourself more time to pause and finish a thought. Applies to the Pipeline voice mode — in Realtime mode the voice model detects the end of your turn itself.

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: How loudly {name} speaks its answers. 100% is the loudest — turn it down if {name} is too loud. Speech is boosted so it carries as loud as music, without distortion.

bar_size.title: Bar size
bar_size.description: How big the on-screen bar is. It scales proportionally — width and height together — so the bar keeps its shape and only its size changes. Drag to resize it live on screen.
bar_size.restart_caption: Saved — applies when the bar restarts.

audio_devices.title: Audio devices
audio_devices.description: Pick where {name}'s voice comes out and which microphone {name} listens with. Automatic picks your headset for you and adapts when devices come and go.
audio_devices.none_found: No audio devices found on this machine — {name} keeps running without sound.

settings_view.title: Settings
settings_view.subtitle: Wake-word, hotkeys, safety policies, autostart.

team_proxy.title: Team mode (shared key proxy)
team_proxy.hint: Route providers through a shared key proxy with a per-user token instead of storing real vendor keys on this machine. Leave off for the normal local-key setup.

realtime_voice.title: Realtime voice (browser)
realtime_voice.description: Full-duplex speech-to-speech in the browser (OpenAI). Off by default.
realtime_voice.unavailable: Needs an OpenAI key. Using standard voice.

jarvis_api.title: Control Key
jarvis_api.description: The one key that protects this {name} install. You enter it to unlock {name} in a browser or from another device, and local agents (Codex, Claude Code) use it to drive {name} through the Control API. It was generated automatically at install time — every install has its own.
jarvis_api.copy_failed_toast: Could not copy the Control Key.
jarvis_api.unlock_hint: The lock screen in the browser asks for exactly this key.
jarvis_api.browser_lock_title: Ask for the key in the browser
jarvis_api.browser_lock_description: Off: browsers on this computer open {name} without the key. Other devices and remote access always need the key.
jarvis_api.browser_lock_confirm_title: Require the Control Key in the browser?
jarvis_api.browser_lock_confirm_body: From now on, opening {name} in a browser will ask for the Control Key above. Copy it somewhere safe first — a fresh browser cannot get in without it. This browser stays signed in.
jarvis_api.browser_lock_on_toast: Browser lock is on. New browsers will need the Control Key.
jarvis_api.browser_lock_off_toast: Browser lock is off. Browsers on this computer get in without the key.
jarvis_api.regenerate_confirm_body: The current key stops working immediately — everywhere. Every browser, device, and agent still using it will need the new key.
jarvis_api.regenerated_toast: A new Control Key was generated. Hand it to every device and agent that used the old one.
jarvis_api.custom_hint: Pick a key you can remember — it replaces the current one immediately, everywhere. At least 12 characters; letters, digits, and . _ ~ - only (no spaces).
jarvis_api.custom_charset: Only letters, digits, and . _ ~ - are allowed (no spaces).
jarvis_api.custom_mismatch: The two entries do not match.
jarvis_api.custom_saved_toast: Your Control Key is set. It now unlocks {name} everywhere.
jarvis_api.usage_hint: For agents: send it as an Authorization: Bearer header to your local {name} (/api/control). GET /api/control/allowlist lists everything you can change.

rows.scope_description: "Work in folder X" pins context until released.
rows.autostart_description: The assistant starts in tray after login.
rows.toasts_description: Show "{name} opened X" notification when voice controls the UI.

wake_word.title: Wake Word
wake_word.description: Activation phrase for voice mode. Type your own.
wake_word.activation_hint: On: Jarvis is always listening for your word (needs a local model). Off: activate with the Call keyboard shortcut.
wake_word.language_callout_title: Choose the language you SPEAK — not where the word comes from.
wake_word.language_hint: Jarvis matches your pronunciation, not the word itself. If you speak German, choose German — even for a name like "Alex" or an English word. Pick the wrong language and Jarvis silently never hears you. This choice is independent of the app's display language — the app can stay in English while your wake word is German.
wake_word.degraded_warning: The chosen phrase isn't supported by the selected engine — it will fall back to a different engine.
wake_word.needs_whisper_hint: This phrase needs a local engine. Install the local speech pack below — until then the wake word stays off and you activate with the Call shortcut.

engine_options.vosk_kws: Keyword spotting (any phrase, same on every device)
engine_options.stt_match: STT match (any phrase, needs local Whisper)

wake_word.enable_local_installing: Installing the local speech pack… this can take a minute.
wake_word.enable_local_done: Local speech pack installed. Your wake word is ready without a restart.
wake_word.enable_local_error: Install failed - the details below name the cause. Most often no prebuilt package exists yet for this Python version or system; only a connection error in the details means a network problem.
wake_word.download_model_done: Wake model downloaded — re-saving your wake word…
wake_word.download_model_error: Could not download the wake model. Check your connection and try again.

wake_word.title: Set your wake word
wake_word.body: Choose a word or short phrase {name} will listen for. You can type anything — for example your name.
wake_word.degraded_note: This phrase isn't supported by the current engine — it will use a fallback. You can still continue.

autostart.title: Launch app at login
autostart.description: Launch the assistant automatically when you log in, so your wake word works right after a reboot. Turn it off here any time.
autostart.enabled_toast: Autostart enabled — {name} will launch at login.
autostart.unsupported: Autostart is not available on this host (e.g. a headless server with no desktop session).
autostart.instant_start_hint: On Windows, instant start uses a scheduled task and asks for permission once. Without it, Windows may delay startup by several minutes on machines with many startup programs.
autostart.instant_start_active: Instant start is active — {name} launches within seconds of login.
autostart.instant_start_enabled_toast: Instant start enabled — {name} will launch right after login.
autostart.instant_start_declined_toast: Permission was declined — {name} still starts via the shortcut (may be delayed).

overlay_style.title: Display style
overlay_style.description: Choose how {name} appears on screen during voice activity. The bar is the slim default; the mascot is the classic orb; or hide it entirely.
overlay_style.label: Style

system_prompt.title: System Prompt
system_prompt.description: The instructions that define how your assistant thinks and speaks. Edit the Markdown to personalize it, or reset to the default at any time.
system_prompt.empty_hint: Can't be empty — use Reset to restore the default.
system_prompt.reset_done: Reset to the default system prompt
system_prompt.applies_next_turn: Applies on your assistant's next message — no restart needed.

keybinds.title: Voice Keybinds
keybinds.description: Set the keys for Call and Hangup. Click Record and press your combination — or click keys on the on-screen keyboard. Changes apply instantly.
keybinds.recording_hint: Hold the keys you want (then let go), or click them on the keyboard below. Esc cancels.

validation.only_modifiers: Add a real key — Ctrl/Alt/Shift alone can't be a trigger.
validation.solo_typing_key: This key fires every time you type — add Ctrl/Alt/Shift or a second key. Function keys (F5) and navigation keys (↑, Home) also work on their own.
validation.windows_reserved: The Windows key is reserved by the system — use Ctrl, Alt or Shift instead.
validation.alt_f4: Alt+F4 closes the active window — choose another combination.
validation.ctrl_c: Ctrl+C is the copy shortcut — choose another combination.
validation.collision: Overlaps with {action} ({combo}) — one press would trigger both. Choose different keys.
validation.solo_nav: Allowed — but note: this key also fires while navigating text.

keyboard.hint: Press keys or click them to build the combo

settings_view.codex_description: Manual path for Windows PATH issues. Empty uses 'codex' from PATH.
settings_view.safety_description: Patterns executed without confirmation, even at risk-tier 'ask'. Avoids confirmation fatigue.

telephony_view.title: Telephony (experimental)
telephony_view.subtitle: Call your assistant on a real phone number. Same STT, Brain, and Charon voice as the local wake-word path. Note: this telephony integration is experimental and has not been tested yet.
telephony_view.not_configured_hint: Set your Twilio Account SID, Auth Token, and phone number below to finish setup, then run "Test connection".
telephony_view.unavailable_body: The optional Twilio integration is not available in this install. Add the telephony extras group, then restart {name}.

creds.title: Credentials & config
creds.greeting_placeholder: Leave empty for the default {name} greeting

scripts.title: Setup scripts
scripts.subtitle: Run these to expose {name} publicly and provision the Twilio number. Copy a command with the button.
scripts.empty: No setup scripts reported by the backend yet.

calls.title: Recent calls
calls.empty: No calls yet. Once someone dials your number, the turns appear here.

telephony_setup.title: Telephony setup
telephony_setup.subtitle: Step-by-step guide and setup scripts for Twilio telephony.
telephony_setup.intro: How to make {name} reachable by phone — from the Twilio number to the first call.
telephony_setup.step1_body: Run `pip install "personal-jarvis[telephony]"` on the server and restart {name}. Only needed if the status above reports the extra is missing.
telephony_setup.step2_body: Sign up at twilio.com, copy your Account SID and Auth Token, and buy a voice-capable phone number.
telephony_setup.step3_body: Twilio must reach {name} over HTTPS. Run the tunnel script below and paste the printed URL as the public base URL.
telephony_setup.step4_body: In the Twilio console, set your number's Voice webhook to the webhook URL shown below.
telephony_setup.step5_body: Back on the API Keys page, enter the Account SID, Auth Token and phone number, enable telephony, then run "Test connection" and "Self-test voice".

previews_view.title: Previews
previews_view.subtitle: Live preview servers {name} spawned for you.

outputs_view.title: Outputs
outputs_view.subtitle: Files {name} or {name}-Agents wrote for you.
outputs_view.needs_review_hint: A deliverable was saved, but no critic approval was recorded. Review the files or restart the mission.
outputs_view.partial_output_hint: Some files were retained from the failed mission. Their presence is not an approval.
outputs_view.empty_sessions: No {name}-Agent sessions yet. Start a {name}-Agent task.
outputs_view.single_shot_no_plan: Single-shot run — no structured plan for this session.
outputs_view.no_files: This session has no saved files.
outputs_view.binary_file_hint: Binary file — open the folder on the desktop to view it.
outputs_view.file_truncated: File truncated to 1 MiB — view the rest in the file manager.
outputs_view.open_with_empty: No apps detected to open this file.

common.open_in_editor: Open Markdown file in OS default editor

sessions.select_one: Select a session on the left.
sessions.no_turns: This session has no turns — probably only a wake-word.

provider_switcher.loading_hint: Provider list is loading — if it stays empty, check the backend.

wiki_provider.description: The Knowledge Wiki uses a dedicated model to write and tidy your long-term notes. Leave the provider on "Same as brain" and the model empty to use a cheap, fast default — or pin a specific provider and model here.
wiki_provider.model_hint: Empty means the chosen provider's cheap/fast model. Pick a model id only to override it.
wiki_provider.load_error: Could not load the Wiki provider settings.
wiki_provider.how_intro: Jarvis maintains its long-term memory automatically. Every maintenance run has three steps:
wiki_provider.how_step1: Read along: during every conversation a cheap model silently reads along and notes candidate facts worth remembering (preferences, names, projects, dates).
wiki_provider.how_step2: Reconcile: a judge pass compares each candidate against the existing wiki pages and decides: create a new page, update an existing one, or discard.
wiki_provider.how_step3: Sweep: when a live voice session ends, the full transcript is reviewed once more so nothing slips through.
wiki_provider.how_model_note: Every step runs on the provider and model you pick here — pick Codex, and Codex really performs the run. Left empty, the brain's provider takes over with its cheap, fast model. If the chosen provider is unreachable, another configured provider steps in automatically so memory keeps working.
wiki_provider.resolved_fallback_warning: No credentials are configured for this provider — the run automatically falls back to another configured provider.

subagent_model.description: {name}-Agents (heavy background workers) can run on their own model, separate from the chat brain. Leave the field empty to use the active {name}-Agent provider's strongest model.
subagent_model.model_placeholder: Leave empty for the provider's default model
subagent_model.model_hint: Empty means the active {name}-Agent provider's deep model.
subagent_model.saved_restart: {name}-Agent model saved — applies to the next mission.

cli_connect.login_aborted: {0}: login check failed after 5 min. Restart manually if needed.

apikeys_codex.needs_codex_full: {0}: first connect Codex or save API key, then activate.
apikeys_codex.needs_key_full: {0}: first save API key, then activate.
apikeys_codex.runs_as_subagent_hint: Codex works as a {name}-Agent (background worker) on your ChatGPT login. After connecting, activate it in the {name}-Agent list below. A thinking brain needs the separate OpenAI API-key provider.
apikeys_codex.brain_needs_openai_key: Codex as a brain needs an OpenAI API key — the ChatGPT login only powers the Codex worker. Add an OpenAI key (e.g. on the OpenAI provider), then select Codex here.
apikeys_codex.brain_key_hint: To use Codex as a brain too, add an OpenAI API key (paid, separate from your ChatGPT login — which only powers the worker). Saving it unlocks the brain toggle above.

apikeys_antigravity.login_started: Google login was started in the terminal
apikeys_antigravity.needs_login_full: {0}: first sign in with Google, then activate.

terminal_xterm.install_no_binary: {0}: install completed (exit 0) but binary not found. PATH may not be refreshed.
terminal_xterm.install_failed: {0}: install failed (exit {1}). Try a different method.

welcome.title: Welcome to Personal Jarvis
welcome.subtitle: Your voice-first AI assistant. Let's get you set up.

tutorial.title: Watch the 2-minute tour
tutorial.body: A quick walkthrough of what Personal Jarvis does and how setup works. You can skip and start right away.

risk.title: Before you continue
risk.lead: Personal Jarvis is powerful, experimental software.
risk.body: It can control your computer, run commands, open apps, send messages, and change files on your behalf — and it can make mistakes. It may do unintended or even destructive things.
risk.liability: You use it entirely at your own risk. The developers accept no liability for any damage, data loss, or other consequences. Only continue if you understand and accept this.
risk.accept_label: I understand the risks and accept that I use Personal Jarvis at my own risk.
risk.declined_body: You declined the Terms, so Personal Jarvis is closing now — nothing was set up and nothing was saved. Start the app again anytime if you change your mind; this screen will ask again.
risk.view_terms: View the full Terms of Use

intro.scene_2: Just talk — it listens and acts.
intro.scene_3: It keeps working in the background while you don't.
intro.scene_4: Your keys, your data, your rules.

terms.title: Terms & Disclaimer
terms.intro: Please read and accept before continuing.
terms.accept_label: I have read and accept the Terms & Disclaimer.

permissions.title: Allow access on this Mac
permissions.body: macOS asks you to approve each capability separately. Use the buttons below, then return here; the status updates automatically.
permissions.privacy_note: These prompts come from macOS. The installer cannot silently approve them, and Personal Jarvis never bypasses System Settings. The Keychain prompt at app start only means Personal Jarvis is saving your API keys encrypted in the macOS Keychain — they never leave your Mac.

wake_word.choice_title: How do you want to activate {name}?
wake_word.choice_body: Pick whichever fits how you work — you can change this later in Settings.
wake_word.mode_wake_body: Say your own word any time — always listening. Needs a small local model for your exact word.
wake_word.mode_shortcut_body: No wake word — press the Call shortcut to start a voice session.
wake_word.shortcut_note: You'll activate {name} with the Call shortcut instead of a wake word. You can configure it anytime in Settings → Voice Keybinds.
wake_word.title: Pick your wake word
wake_word.body: Choose the word that wakes your assistant. "Hey" stays fixed — you pick the rest.
wake_word.notice: Pick any word — just make sure it isn't someone else's trademark.
wake_word.references_caveat: These references are informational and may be incomplete or out of date — they are not a substitute for your own check.
wake_word.ack_label: I'm responsible for the wake word I choose.

mic_check.title: Microphone check
mic_check.good: Sounds good — your microphone picked up your voice clearly.
mic_check.too_quiet: That was very quiet. Turn up your microphone volume or move closer, then try again.
mic_check.no_device: No microphone detected — you can still finish setup and fix this later in Settings.
mic_check.permission_required: Grant Microphone access in Permissions before running this check.
mic_check.error: Could not check the microphone right now.

api_keys.title: Set up API keys after onboarding
api_keys.body: Finish onboarding first. Then open API Keys & Providers. You do not need to fill every API-key field.
api_keys.screenshot_alt: Personal Jarvis API Keys view with the API Keys navigation and Realtime or Pipeline switch highlighted
api_keys.api_location_body: After onboarding, select API Keys in the left sidebar. Paste provider keys only in this secure view.
api_keys.realtime_body: Add one OpenAI or Gemini key for listening, reasoning, and speech in one model.
api_keys.pipeline_body: Add keys only for the Brain, Voice Output, and Voice Input providers you choose.
api_keys.security_note: Paste keys only into the secure API Keys view — never into chat or voice.

mic_test.title: Microphone check
mic_test.body: Let's make sure your assistant can hear you.
mic_test.no_mic: No microphone detected. You can still talk by text or through a channel like Telegram.

system_style.title: Choose your style
system_style.body: How should your assistant appear on screen? You can change this any time in Settings.

captions.jarvis_bar: A slim floating voice bar, always within reach.
captions.mascot: A floating companion orb that reacts as you talk.
captions.none: Just the app window — no floating surface on screen.

system_style.needs_restart: Almost there — this style needs a quick restart to take effect.
system_style.save_error: Couldn't save that — the recommended default stays. You can change it later in Settings.

finish.title: You're all set!
finish.body: Your assistant is ready. Try saying your wake word, or type a command to begin.
finish.skipped_title: You skipped — set up anytime in Settings:
finish.boot_notice: Heads up: the first launch takes about 10 seconds while your assistant warms up — that's completely normal.

languages_view.title: Languages
languages_view.subtitle: Choose the UI language and the language {name} replies in.
languages_view.ui_hint: Affects every label, button and message in the app.
languages_view.reply_hint: Which language {name} speaks when answering you. Proper names, code identifiers and technical terms stay in their original form.
languages_view.stt_hint: Which language your spoken voice is recognised and turned into text. Automatic detects it for you. After changing this, end any Realtime call and restart Jarvis for Pipeline mode.

en.label: English
en.description: Use English for every label and button.

de.label: Deutsch (German)
de.description: Use German for every label and button.

es.label: Español (Spanish)
es.description: Use Spanish for every label and button.

reply_options.auto: {name} mirrors whichever language you speak or type.

stt_options.auto: Detect the language you speak automatically.
stt_options.en: Always recognise your voice as English.
stt_options.de: Always recognise your voice as German.
stt_options.es: Always recognise your voice as Spanish.

cli_connect_coach.instructions: Follow the instructions in the terminal on the left. I check every 3 seconds whether the login went through.

frontier_switch_modal.description: detected a newer model at the provider on startup and switched automatically. Please confirm.

pair_dialog.accept_hint: Paste the pair link your friend sent you here.

session_list.empty_hint: Say your wake word — when you hang up, the session shows up here.

wiki_search.no_hits_suffix: . Try other keywords or look in the graph.

wiki_graph.empty_prefix: Your memory graph is still empty. As soon as
wiki_graph.empty_suffix: creates notes, the nodes will connect here automatically.

obsidian_button.not_installed_hint: — if Obsidian does not start, it is not installed.

obsidian_setup_dialog.restart_obsidian_hint: Please open Obsidian once and close it again — then click again.
obsidian_setup_dialog.title: Set up Obsidian
obsidian_setup_dialog.subtitle: Three small steps and your wiki is live in Obsidian.
obsidian_setup_dialog.install_body_1: {name} writes the wiki as Markdown files to disk.
obsidian_setup_dialog.install_body_2: To browse and edit them like a real knowledge base, you need the free editor
obsidian_setup_dialog.connect_body_1: Now {name} registers its wiki in your Obsidian configuration.
obsidian_setup_dialog.connect_body_2: This makes it appear in the vault switcher and the
obsidian_setup_dialog.live_test_body: Click the button — your browser opens the vault directly in Obsidian.
obsidian_setup_dialog.mode_separate_hint: The default — {name} keeps its own vault, isolated from your other notes.
obsidian_setup_dialog.mode_existing_hint: {name} writes into a "Jarvis" subfolder inside the vault you pick.
obsidian_setup_dialog.mode_existing_disabled_hint: No existing vaults found — register one in Obsidian first.
obsidian_setup_dialog.restart_required_hint: Restart {name} to start writing to the new location.

docs_overview.title: Personal Jarvis docs
docs_overview.description: Everything from first setup to the architecture behind the voice. Browse the complete local library or open the redesigned public documentation.
docs_overview.load_failed_description: The local index did not become ready in time. Try again without restarting the app.
docs_overview.empty_description: The online documentation is still available from the button above.
docs_overview.local_library_description: Fast, offline access to the documentation shipped with this installation.
docs_overview.indexing_description: Scanning the local Markdown library and building full-text search. This usually takes only a moment.
docs_overview.browse_description: Follow a learning path or jump to the feature you need.

global_kill_button.cancel_all_body: running mission(s) will be hard-stopped. Worker subprocesses are terminated via the Job Object; diffs already written stay in the worktrees.

mission_tree.empty: No missions yet. The main assistant will dispatch one shortly…

event_timeline.empty: No events for this mission yet.

verdict_panel.empty: No critic verdicts for this mission yet.

jarvis_agent_panel.select_mission: Select a mission to see {name}-Agent workers.

clis_view.intro_can_call: can call these CLIs as tools. Only
clis_view.intro_appear: CLIs appear in the brain tool catalog.
clis_view.follow_browser_login: follow the browser login. Status is checked automatically.
clis_view.api_key_help: Keys are stored in the Windows Credential Manager and injected as an ENV variable on each call.
clis_view.install_help: Opens an external Windows terminal with the install command. The status is checked automatically once the terminal finishes.
clis_view.install_running: install is running. Status updates automatically.
clis_view.field_binary_name: Binary name (what is on the PATH)
clis_view.empty_custom: No custom CLI registered yet — click 'Add Custom' at the top right.

cli_test_hub_view.subtitle_rest: in plain language what to do with your CLIs
cli_test_hub_view.no_cli_help: can only call a tool once at least one CLI is connected (e.g. gcloud, gh, docker). Connect a CLI in the CLIs section — it then appears here automatically.
cli_test_hub_view.placeholder_rest: what to do with your CLIs…
cli_test_hub_view.picking_tool: is picking a tool and running the command…
cli_test_hub_view.backend_check_prefix: Is the backend server running and the endpoint
cli_test_hub_view.no_tool_found: found no matching CLI tool. Phrase the instruction more concretely or give a CLI hint.

workflows_view.subtitle: Your hosted AI-agent pipelines — cron-triggered or at the push of a button.
workflows_view.empty_title: No workflows yet — 3 examples are seeded on first launch.

conductor_view.editor_intro: Jobs are JSON — copyable, diffable, git-friendly. Three types:
conductor_view.empty_jobs: No jobs yet. Click "New job" in the top right.
conductor_view.empty_jobs_hint: 3 demo jobs are seeded on first launch.
conductor_view.empty_timeline: No runs yet — click Play on a job.

chat_tab.empty_hint: No friends yet. Click the top-right
chat_tab.select_hint: Pick a friend from the list on the left.

status_tab.heading: Status sharing
status_tab.intro_part1: You decide per friend how much of your
status_tab.intro_part2: status is shared. Privacy-first: the default is
status_tab.empty_hint: No friends yet. Switch to the Chat tab and click
status_tab.hard_blacklist_body: Raw utterances, tool args, stack traces, memory updates, window titles. These NEVER leave your machine — no matter which profile is active and no matter what a custom whitelist says.
status_tab.profile_minimal_desc: Friend sees: voice session active yes/no, duration. No content.
status_tab.profile_standard_desc: Friend also sees: which missions/tasks are currently running, success, duration.
status_tab.profile_detailed_desc: Friend also sees: signed {name}-Agent summaries (NO utterances).

tools_canvas.no_tools_explainer: As soon as the {name}-Agent worker triggers a tool (e.g. dispatch_to_harness, run_shell, screenshot), it appears here as an icon node.

chat_thread.no_channel_placeholder: No channel linked — outbound not possible
chat_thread.link_channel_hint: Link a channel (Telegram/Jarvis) to start writing

add_friend_menu.title: Add friend
add_friend_menu.link_mode_hint: Pair-Link uses the existing public-key pairing flow via the board backend. Exchange the token + URL with your friend.

self_mod_view.intro_a: Read-only view of the audit trail, mutable settings and backups.
self_mod_view.intro_b: Mutations happen only via voice/chat — this view shows only what

wiki_view.load_error: Could not load wiki data. Is the backend server running?
wiki_view.no_selection_hint: Pick a page on the left or click a node in the Memory Map.
wiki_view.backlinks_hint: Backlinks appear here once a page is selected.
wiki_view.empty_body_b: picks up something important in a conversation, it lands here — people, projects, preferences, appointments.

wiki_health.capture_aria: Wiki capture funnel for the last {0} hours
wiki_health.pending_writes: {0} facts are waiting to be written
wiki_health.reindex_failed: The wiki search index could not be rebuilt.

skill_create_dialog.title: Create skill
skill_create_dialog.subtitle: Describe what the skill should do — draft it with AI or fill the form yourself.
skill_create_dialog.intent_placeholder: e.g. "Summarize my open browser tabs", "Start my morning routine"…
skill_create_dialog.brain_used: AI draft applied — review and edit below.
skill_create_dialog.brain_unavailable: AI unavailable — filled a starter template you can edit.
skill_create_dialog.description_placeholder: One sentence about what it does
skill_create_dialog.body_placeholder:
## My Skill

What the assistant should do…
skill_create_dialog.name_required: Please enter a name with at least 3 characters.
skill_create_dialog.needs_instructions: Please describe what the skill should do — type it in the field at the top, or write the instructions below.
skill_create_dialog.write_with_ai: Write the whole skill with AI
skill_create_dialog.ai_done_review: Done — the AI wrote the whole skill below. Review it, then click Create skill.

missions_view.select_worker_hint: Select a worker in the tree on the left to see the live stream.
missions_view.select_mission_hint: Select a mission to load details.
missions_view.title: Missions
missions_view.subtitle: Phase 6 — Worker/Critic loop. Live bus, PTY streams, verdicts.
missions_view.no_events: No events for this mission yet.
missions_view.no_critic_verdicts: No critic verdicts for this mission yet.
missions_view.no_missions_yet: No missions yet. Main Jarvis will dispatch one shortly…

mission_tool_approvals.select_mission: Select a mission to review supervisor tool requests.
mission_tool_approvals.toast_pending: Approval needed for {tool} in mission {mission}. Open Missions to review it.
mission_tool_approvals.empty_body: This mission has no supervisor tool call awaiting your decision.
mission_tool_approvals.scope_notice: Each approval applies only to this exact paused call. Future calls are reviewed separately.
mission_tool_approvals.expired_body: This request can no longer be decided. The list will refresh automatically.
mission_tool_approvals.reason_plausibility: The action did not match the request with enough confidence.
mission_tool_approvals.confirm_body: This authorizes exactly one execution with the arguments shown above.

skill_finder_dialog.title: Skill Finder
skill_finder_dialog.subtitle: Describe what you need — the mini-agent searches the catalog and ranks with AI.
skill_finder_dialog.no_direct_download: No direct download available — install manually
skill_finder_dialog.empty_prompt: Describe what you need — the search ranks with AI.
skill_finder_dialog.example_1: “build a skill that prioritizes my emails”

agents_view.subtitle: Running {name}-Agents — role, status, output.
agents_view.empty_body_b: starts a {name}-Agent, it appears here as a live tile with streaming output and a kill button.

friends_view.subtitle: Telegram bot + local friend registry. You decide what friends see.

feedback.discord_cta_subtitle: Open #report-a-bug and post your bug, idea, or question directly.
feedback.subtitle: Open our Discord to report a bug, share an idea, or ask a question.
feedback.description_placeholder: Tell us more — steps to reproduce, context, expected vs. actual behaviour…
feedback.result_not_configured: Feedback channel isn't set up yet — nothing was sent.
feedback.result_error: Couldn't reach Discord, please try again later.
